followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use DBACOCKPIT for AMDP development?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP development ke dauran DBACOCKPIT SQLScript logic ko AMDP method mein embed karne se pehle HANA database mein directly prototype aur validate karne ke liye use hota hai. DBACOCKPIT ke andar SQL Console SQLScript queries run karne, JOINs test karne, aggregation results verify karne, aur performance check karne deta hai. Yahan pehle logic validate karke, AMDP development cycle faster hota hai — sirf tested, working SQLScript class ke andar place hota hai, activation aur debugging iterations reduce karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: DBACOCKPIT is used during AMDP development to prototype and validate SQLScript logic directly in the HANA database before embedding it in an AMDP method. The SQL Console within DBACOCKPIT allows running SQLScript queries, testing JOINs, verifying aggregation results, and checking performance. By validating the logic here first, the AMDP development cycle is faster — only tested, working SQLScript is placed inside the class, reducing activation and debugging iterations.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Complex aging calculation likhna tha. ABAP class mein seedha likhna risky tha — syntax errors, logic issues. DBACOCKPIT SQL Console mein pehle 2 hours spend ki — full SQLScript test kiya with sample data. Results correct aaye. Phir that exact script AMDP method mein paste kiya — first activation pass hua. Yeh workflow bahut time save karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: DBACOCKPIT skip karna aur directly AMDP mein likh ke trial-and-error karna — har syntax error ke baad activation failure, HANA error message parse karna. DBACOCKPIT se pehle test karo — saves multiple iteration cycles.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" DBACOCKPIT SQL Console workflow:
" TCode: DBACOCKPIT
" Path: Diagnostics -> SQL Console

" Step 1: Test base query
SELECT lifnr, dmbtr, bldat
FROM bsik
WHERE bukrs = '1000'
AND gjahr = '2024';
-- Check: correct rows? expected columns?

" Step 2: Test with tabular variable
lt_open = SELECT lifnr, dmbtr,
                 DAYS_BETWEEN(bldat, CURRENT_DATE) AS age
          FROM bsik WHERE bukrs = '1000';
SELECT * FROM :lt_open ORDER BY age DESC;
-- Check: age calculation correct?

" Step 3: Add CASE WHEN
SELECT lifnr, dmbtr, age,
       CASE WHEN age <= 30 THEN '0-30'
            WHEN age <= 60 THEN '31-60'
            ELSE '60+' END AS bucket
FROM :lt_open;
-- Check: buckets correct?
" Step 4: Copy verified script -> paste into AMDP

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between ST05 and SE30 for AMDP performance analysis?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST05 SQL level aur database level pe trace karta hai — yeh har database call dikhata hai, kaunsi tables accessed hui, kitni rows fetched hui, aur per SQL statement execution time. AMDP validation ke liye, ST05 transferred rows mein reduction dikhata hai: AMDP se pehle thousands of rows application server pe aati hain; AMDP ke baad sirf aggregated result set trace mein appear karta hai. SE30 ABAP program level pe trace karta hai — yeh total runtime ko ABAP processing time aur database time mein break karke dikhata hai. AMDP processing ko database pe shift karta hai, isliye ABAP time proportion decrease hona chahiye aur DB time increase jabki total runtime significantly drop hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ST05 traces at the SQL and database level — it shows each database call, which tables were accessed, how many rows were fetched, and execution time per SQL statement. For AMDP validation, ST05 shows the reduction in rows transferred: before AMDP thousands of rows come to the application server; after AMDP only the aggregated result set appears in the trace. SE30 traces at the ABAP program level — it shows total runtime broken into ABAP processing time and database time. AMDP shifts processing to the database, so the ABAP time proportion should decrease and DB time should increase while total runtime drops significantly.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Report optimization ke baad dono traces liye. ST05: before 480,000 rows fetched, after 48 rows. SE30: before 4 min 20 sec total (3% DB, 97% ABAP), after 9 sec total (82% DB, 18% ABAP). ST05 ne transfer reduction prove kiya, SE30 ne runtime improvement prove kiya. Client ko dono screenshots mile — indisputable proof of improvement.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf SE30 lena aur ST05 skip karna — SE30 overall time dekhata hai, but ST05 specifically data transfer reduction prove karta hai jo HANA pushdown ka core benefit hai. Management ko rows transferred ki reduction visually impact karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST05 Usage for AMDP before/after comparison:
" TCode ST05 -> Trace On
" Run the report being optimized
" TCode ST05 -> Trace Off -> Display Trace
" BEFORE AMDP: expect to see many rows per SQL call
" AFTER AMDP: expect to see one call, very few rows

" ST05 Columns to examine:
" - Records: rows returned per SQL call
" - Time: duration per call
" - Program: which ABAP triggered the call
" - Table: which DB table was hit

" SE30 Usage:
" TCode SE30 -> Settings (choose relevant variant)
" -> Execute -> Run report -> Evaluate
" Key metrics in SE30 output:
" - Database Requests: number of DB calls
" - Database time vs ABAP time split
" BEFORE AMDP: DB time < 20%, ABAP time > 80% (inefficient)
" AFTER AMDP:  DB time > 70%, ABAP time < 30% (pushdown)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use SE11 in the context of AMDP development?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP development mein SE11 do main purposes ke liye use hota hai. Pehla, AMDP method parameters ke liye DDIC-based structure aur table types define karna — IMPORTING aur EXPORTING parameters proper type safety aur transportability ke liye Dictionary-based types use karne chahiye. Doosra, AMDP body ke andar accurate SQLScript likhne ke liye transparent table field names, types, aur lengths look up karna. DDIC check karna yeh bhi reveal karta hai ki field foreign key hai ya optimization logic ke liye relevant value table constraints hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE11 is used in AMDP development for two main purposes. First, defining DDIC-based structure and table types for AMDP method parameters — IMPORTING and EXPORTING parameters should use Dictionary-based types for proper type safety and transportability. Second, looking up transparent table field names, types, and lengths to write accurate SQLScript inside the AMDP body. Checking DDIC also reveals if a field is a foreign key or has value table constraints relevant to the optimization logic.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP mein BSEG table use karna tha. BSEG cluster table hai — SE11 mein check kiya. Pata chala BSEG HANA mein transparently accessible hai S/4HANA mein (cluster tables eliminated). SE11 se field names confirm kiye — DMBTR, BUKRS, KOSTL exact names verify kiye. Ek wrong field name SQLScript mein runtime error deta hai — SE11 cross-check essential hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AMDP parameters ke liye local ABAP types (TYPES: BEGIN OF ...) use karna — yeh technically work kar sakta hai simple cases mein lekin DDIC-based types preferred hain for transportability and type checking. SE11 mein proper types banao.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE11 usage for AMDP development:

" Step 1: Check table structure in SE11
" SE11 -> Database Table -> BSIK -> Display
" Note field names exactly: LIFNR, BUKRS, DMBTR, BLDAT
" Note data types: LIFNR = CHAR10, DMBTR = CURR...
" -> Use exact same names in SQLScript

" Step 2: Create structure for AMDP result
" SE11 -> Data Type -> Create -> Structure
" Name: ZST_VENDOR_AGING_RESULT
" Fields: LIFNR TYPE LIFNR
"         NAME1 TYPE NAME1_GP
"         DMBTR TYPE DMBTR
"         AGE_DAYS TYPE I
"         BUCKET TYPE CHAR10
" Activate

" Step 3: Create table type
" SE11 -> Data Type -> Create -> Table Type
" Name: ZTT_VENDOR_AGING_RESULT
" Line Type: ZST_VENDOR_AGING_RESULT
" Activate
" -> Use ZTT_VENDOR_AGING_RESULT as EXPORTING param type

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you find the stored procedure created by AMDP in HANA?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab AMDP class activate hoti hai, SAP automatically HANA mein corresponding stored procedure create karta hai. Procedure name format follow karta hai: PACKAGE_NAME::CLASS_NAME=>METHOD_NAME. Find karne ke liye, SAP HANA Database Explorer use karo — HANA system se connect karo, SAP system ke use kiye gaye schema pe navigate karo, aur Procedures ke andar search karo. Procedure body mein AMDP method mein likha gaya SQLScript hota hai. Yahan AMDP debugging bhi hoti hai — HANA Database Explorer ke through procedure mein breakpoints set karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When an AMDP class is activated, SAP automatically creates a corresponding stored procedure in HANA. The procedure name follows the format: PACKAGE_NAME::CLASS_NAME=>METHOD_NAME. To find it, use SAP HANA Database Explorer — connect to the HANA system, navigate to the schema used by the SAP system, and search under Procedures. The procedure body contains the SQLScript written in the AMDP method. This is also where AMDP debugging happens — set breakpoints in the procedure via HANA Database Explorer.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production mein AMDP result unexpected tha. HANA Database Explorer mein stored procedure find ki — ABAP/SAP::ZCL_VENDOR_AGING_AMDP=>GET_VENDOR_AGING. Procedure directly execute ki with test parameters — wrong COALESCE default value mili. AMDP mein fix kiya, reactivate kiya, procedure updated ho gayi. Debugging directly procedure pe was faster than guessing.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Yeh assume karna ki AMDP procedure HANA mein manually create karni padti hai — nahi, SAP activation automatically banata hai. Aur yeh assume karna ki procedure ko manually HANA mein modify kar sakte ho — mat karo, next AMDP activation overwrite karega. Changes hamesha ABAP AMDP class mein karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Finding AMDP stored procedure in HANA:

" Method 1: HANA Database Explorer
" 1. Open HANA DB Explorer
" 2. Connect to HANA system (same as SAP system)
" 3. Navigate: Database -> Catalog -> Schema (SAPHANADB)
" 4. Procedures -> Search: ZCL_VENDOR_AGING
" 5. Find: ABAP/SAP::ZCL_VENDOR_AGING_AMDP=>GET_VENDOR_AGING
" 6. Right-click -> Open -> see SQLScript body

" Method 2: DBACOCKPIT SQL Console
SELECT procedure_name, definition
FROM procedures
WHERE procedure_name LIKE '%VENDOR_AGING%'
  AND schema_name = CURRENT_SCHEMA;

" Method 3: SE24 -> AMDP class -> Method
" Additional menu -> Where Used -> HANA Procedures
" (some versions show the procedure link directly)

" Naming convention:
" ZCL_VENDOR_AGING_AMDP class + GET_VENDOR_AGING method
" -> Procedure: ABAP/SAP::ZCL_VENDOR_AGING_AMDP=>GET_VENDOR_AGING

followupAnswerBank.question: What transaction do you use to transport AMDP objects?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP objects standard SAP Workbench Transport ke through SE09 ya SE10 use karke transport hote hain. AMDP class ek ABAP class hai aur SE24 mein activate hone par automatically transport request mein capture hoti hai. SE11 mein created DDIC types separate transport requests ya same request mein jaate hain. Transport sequence respect karna padta hai — DDIC types pehle, phir AMDP class. HANA stored procedure separately transport nahi hoti — yeh target system mein automatically recreated hoti hai jab AMDP class wahan activate hoti hai. ADT mein developed CDS table functions bhi SE09/SE10 mein appear karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: AMDP objects are transported using the standard SAP Workbench Transport via SE09 or SE10. The AMDP class is an ABAP class and is captured in a transport request automatically when activated in SE24. DDIC types created in SE11 go into separate transport requests or the same request. The transport sequence must be respected — DDIC types first, then the AMDP class. The HANA stored procedure is not transported separately — it is automatically recreated in the target system when the AMDP class is activated there. CDS table functions developed in ADT also appear in SE09/SE10.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Transport ke baad target system mein AMDP activate nahi hui — error: USING clause mein referenced CDS view not found. Check kiya — CDS view transport request alag tha aur transport nahi hua tha. Correct sequence: first CDS view request import, then AMDP class request. SE09 mein request dependencies set kar sakte ho — isko use karo overlapping transports mein.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AMDP class transport karna aur DDIC types transport bhoolna — target mein activation failure. Aur CDS table function transport sequence miss karna — agar AMDP table function implement karta hai toh CDS object pehle transport hona chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Transport sequence for AMDP objects:
" SE09 / SE10 -> display transport requests

" Correct order (must be imported in this sequence):
" Request 1: DDIC Objects
"   - ZST_VENDOR_AGING_RESULT (structure)
"   - ZTT_VENDOR_AGING_RESULT (table type)

" Request 2: CDS Objects (if table function used)
"   - ZI_BUKRS (CDS interface view used in USING)
"   - ZTF_VENDOR_AGING (CDS table function)

" Request 3: AMDP Class
"   - ZCL_VENDOR_AGING_AMDP (class + methods)

" After import in target system:
" - Class activates
" - SAP automatically creates stored procedure in HANA
" - No separate HANA deployment needed

" To set transport dependencies in SE09:
" Transport request -> Attributes -> Predecessor Transports
" Link Request 3 depends on Request 2 depends on Request 1

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use ST12 for AMDP performance profiling?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST12 ABAP performance profiling mein method-level granularity provide karta hai — yeh call hierarchy aur har method mein spent time dikhata hai, including AMDP method calls. AMDP optimization ke baad, ST12 run karne se exactly reveal hota hai ki AMDP call kitna time leti hai versus calling ABAP code. Yeh identify karne mein help karta hai ki remaining performance issues SQLScript logic ke andar hain ya results process karne wale ABAP wrapper code mein. ST12 SE30 se zyada detailed hai diagnose karne ke liye ki kaunsa specific method bottleneck hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ST12 provides method-level granularity in ABAP performance profiling — it shows the call hierarchy and time spent in each method, including AMDP method calls. After AMDP optimization, running ST12 reveals exactly how much time the AMDP call takes versus the calling ABAP code. This helps identify whether remaining performance issues are inside the SQLScript logic or in the ABAP wrapper code processing the results. ST12 is more detailed than SE30 for diagnosing which specific method is the bottleneck.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP ke baad overall time 9 seconds tha — better tha but client expectation 3 seconds thi. ST12 se dekha: AMDP call itself 1.2 seconds, but ABAP loop processing returned result set 7.8 seconds le raha tha. Issue AMDP mein nahi tha — calling ABAP mein tha. ST12 ne correctly pinpoint kiya — without it hum AMDP ko unnecessarily tune karte.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf AMDP ko blame karna jab overall performance problem hai — ST12 se pehle check karo ki slowness actually AMDP ke andar hai ya bahar. AMDP sirf ek part hai complete flow ka.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST12 Usage for AMDP profiling:
" TCode: ST12
" 1. Activate Trace: ST12 -> ABAP Trace -> Activate
" 2. Set: User = your user, Program = report to analyze
" 3. Run the report/transaction
" 4. ST12 -> Evaluate -> Workload Overview

" What to look for in ST12 output:
" - Call hierarchy tree
" - Each method with: Net time, Gross time, # calls
" - Find your AMDP method: ZCL_VENDOR_AGING_AMDP
"   -> GET_VENDOR_AGING time = actual AMDP execution time

" Interpreting results:
" AMDP method time = 1 sec -> SQLScript fast (good)
" Calling code time = 8 sec -> fix the ABAP wrapper

" vs SE30: SE30 = total program time + DB/ABAP split
"          ST12 = per-method breakdown (more specific)
" Use both: SE30 for overall picture, ST12 for diagnosis

followupAnswerBank.question: Which transaction is used to view HANA-specific execution plans?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: HANA execution plans DBACOCKPIT SQL Console ya SAP HANA Database Explorer ke through view hote hain. SQLScript query se pehle EXPLAIN PLAN FOR use karne se dikhata hai HANA kaise execute karega — kaunsa access path, join strategy, aur parallelism. AMDP stored procedures ke liye, HANA Database Explorer procedure ke liye visual execution plan provide karta hai. Yeh advanced optimization ke liye use hota hai — identify karna ki index lookup ke bajaye table scan ho raha hai ya specific join order bottleneck cause kar raha hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: HANA execution plans are viewed via DBACOCKPIT SQL Console or SAP HANA Database Explorer. Using EXPLAIN PLAN FOR before a SQLScript query shows how HANA will execute it — which access path, join strategy, and parallelism. For AMDP stored procedures, HANA Database Explorer provides a visual execution plan for the procedure. This is used for advanced optimization — identifying if a table scan is happening instead of index lookup, or if a specific join order is causing a bottleneck.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP ek bade table pe full table scan kar raha tha — ST05 mein time excessive tha. HANA DB Explorer mein execution plan dekha — column store table scan without index. DBA se poochhchhkar pata chala specific column pe index create karna possible tha. Index create kiya, AMDP same code, performance 10x better. Execution plan ne root cause dikhaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Execution plan analysis skip karna aur SQLScript rewrite karte rehna without knowing actual bottleneck — kabhi kabhi issue SQLScript logic mein nahi, table access pattern mein hota hai. Execution plan pehle dekho.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Viewing execution plan via DBACOCKPIT SQL Console:
" TCode: DBACOCKPIT -> Diagnostics -> SQL Console

" Syntax: EXPLAIN PLAN FOR <your query>
EXPLAIN PLAN FOR
SELECT lifnr, SUM(dmbtr) AS total
FROM bsik
WHERE bukrs = '1000'
  AND gjahr = '2024'
GROUP BY lifnr;

" After executing: view plan table
SELECT * FROM EXPLAIN_PLAN_TABLE
ORDER BY POSITION;

" Key columns in plan output:
" - OPERATOR_NAME: Join type, Aggregation type, Table Scan
" - TABLE_NAME: which table accessed
" - ACCESS_PATH: Column Store Scan vs Index Seek
" - ESTIMATED_RECORD_COUNT: rows expected at each step

" Red flags to look for:
" - Full Column Store Table Scan on large table
"   -> consider adding secondary index via SE11/HANA
" - Nested Loop Join on large tables
"   -> consider query restructuring

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of ADT (Eclipse) in AMDP and CDS development?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ADT — Eclipse mein ABAP Development Tools — CDS view development ke liye mandatory environment hai. CDS DDL source files, DCL access control files, aur CDS table functions exclusively ADT mein create hote hain, SE24 ya SE80 mein nahi. AMDP class development ADT aur SE24 dono mein ho sakta hai, lekin ADT advantages provide karta hai: real-time syntax checking, code completion, object definitions par F3 navigation, aur CDS views ke liye F8 data preview. CDS table functions ke saath AMDP combine karne wale projects ke liye, ADT unified development environment hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ADT — ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse — is the mandatory environment for CDS view development. CDS DDL source files, DCL access control files, and CDS table functions are exclusively created in ADT, not in SE24 or SE80. AMDP class development can be done in both ADT and SE24, but ADT provides advantages: real-time syntax checking, code completion, F3 navigation to object definitions, and F8 data preview for CDS views. For projects combining AMDP with CDS table functions, ADT is the unified development environment.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Humare project mein CDS table function + AMDP combination tha. CDS DDL ADT mein banaya, AMDP class bhi ADT mein likhi — same Eclipse window mein dono. F8 se CDS table function test kiya — data directly dikhaya. SE24 mein CDS views open hi nahi hote. ADT mandatory tha modern development ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: CDS views banana SE80 ya SE24 mein try karna — yeh tools classic DDIC ke liye hain, CDS DDL ke liye nahi. CDS development exclusively ADT (Eclipse) mein hota hai. Yeh fundamental setup requirement hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ADT Setup and Key Features for AMDP/CDS development:

" 1. Install ADT: Eclipse -> Help -> Install New Software
"    URL: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest
"    Install: ABAP Development Tools

" 2. Create ABAP Project: connect to SAP system
"    File -> New -> ABAP Project -> enter system details

" 3. Create CDS View in ADT:
"    File -> New -> Other -> Core Data Services -> DDL Source
"    Provides: syntax highlighting, template generation

" 4. Develop AMDP in ADT:
"    File -> New -> ABAP Class -> add IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB
"    SQLScript inside method has syntax support

" 5. Key shortcuts in ADT:
"    F3   = Navigate to definition (table, CDS view)
"    F8   = Data Preview for CDS view
"    Ctrl+Space = Code completion
"    Ctrl+F2    = Syntax Check
"    Ctrl+F3    = Activate

" 6. CDS Table Function in ADT:
"    New -> DDL Source -> Template: Table Function
"    Links to AMDP class via 'implemented by method'

amdp-hana-019.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Interview mein AMDP poochha — phir interviewer aage badhega aur related sawaal puchega. Yeh follow-up questions check karte hain ki tumhare paas sirf surface knowledge hai ya depth hai. Jaise: kisi doctor ko surgery poochho — pehle basic poochha, phir complications, phir alternatives, phir real case. AMDP mein bhi: pehle kya hai, phir performance details, phir authorization, phir CDS comparison, phir debugging. In sab ke liye ready rehna chahiye. Yeh questions predict karna possible hai kyunki sab connected hain ek hi topic se.
amdp-hana-019.hinglishMasterExplanation:
AMDP aur HANA Optimization interview mein follow-up questions ka pattern predictable hai. Category-wise dekho:

1. Technical Deep-Dives: AMDP class structure kaise define karte ho? IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB kya karta hai? USING clause kyun zaruri hai? OPTIONS READ-ONLY kya karta hai? BY DATABASE PROCEDURE vs BY DATABASE FUNCTION difference kya hai? Yeh basic technical questions hain — confident answer karo.

2. SQLScript Questions: Tabular variable kya hai? Set-based vs row-based difference? COALESCE kyun use karte ho? HANA ke date functions — DAYS_BETWEEN, ADD_MONTHS? Loop anti-pattern kyun hai? Yeh HANA knowledge depth check karte hain.

3. Performance Questions: Code pushdown kya hai? ST05 aur SE30 mein kya difference hai? Performance baseline kaise capture karo? Kitna improvement mila real project mein? Execution plan kaise dekhte ho? Numbers ke saath answer karo.

4. CDS Comparison: AMDP vs CDS view kab use karo? CDS table function kya hai? CDS table function ke andar AMDP kaise kaam karta hai? Association vs JOIN difference? Annotation support AMDP mein kyun nahi hai?

5. Authorization Questions: AMDP ke andar AUTHORITY-CHECK kyun nahi hota? Auth kaise handle karo AMDP ke saath? CDS DCL kya hai? Yeh security consciousness check karta hai.

6. Debugging Questions: AMDP debug kaise karo? HANA Database Explorer kaise use karte ho? Stored procedure ka naam HANA mein kaisa hota hai? Intermediate results kaise trace karo?

7. Real Project Questions: Ek real AMDP optimization batao jahan significant improvement mila. Kitna data volume tha? Kya challenges aaye? Transport kaise kiya? Team ko kaise train kiya?

8. Architecture Questions: AMDP kab use karni chahiye kab nahi? Kaunsi cheezein AMDP mein possible nahi hain? ABAP SELECT vs AMDP — tradeoffs? RAP mein AMDP kaise use hoti hai?

9. Comparison with Alternatives: CDS table function se directly SQL run karna vs AMDP — difference? HANA calculation view vs AMDP — kab kya use karo? Native HANA procedure vs AMDP — managed vs unmanaged?

10. Estimation aur Documentation: Effort estimate kaise karte ho? Performance SLA kaise define karte ho? Technical design document mein kya hota hai? Change log kaise maintain karte ho?
amdp-hana-019.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Common follow-up questions on AMDP include: differences between AMDP and CDS views, how authorization is handled since AUTHORITY-CHECK cannot be inside AMDP, how to debug an AMDP when the standard ABAP debugger does not work, what CDS table functions are and how they bridge AMDP to the OData ecosystem, and the anti-pattern of using LOOPs in SQLScript. I prepare for these by having concrete examples from real projects — specific performance numbers like reducing runtime from 4 minutes to 8 seconds, specific transactions used like ST05 and DBACOCKPIT, and clear explanations of architectural decisions like why CDS was chosen over AMDP for a particular use case.'

Hinglish tip: Follow-up questions ka best preparation yeh hai ki ek real project scenario yaad rakho — AMDP kab use kiya, kya problem tha, kya result mila. Uss ek scenario se almost sab follow-up questions cover ho jaate hain. 'Real mein kya kiya' bolna 'theory mein kya hota hai' se hamesha zyada powerful hai.
amdp-hana-019.codeExamples:
" === Common Follow-up Q&A Code Patterns ===

" Follow-up: BY DATABASE PROCEDURE vs BY DATABASE FUNCTION
" PROCEDURE: used for regular AMDP methods called from ABAP
METHOD get_data
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING vbak.
  et_result = SELECT * FROM vbak WHERE erdat > '20240101';
ENDMETHOD.

" FUNCTION: used specifically for CDS Table Functions
" (CDS Table Function backed AMDP uses FUNCTION not PROCEDURE)
METHOD get_data_for_cds
    BY DATABASE FUNCTION FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING vbak.
  et_result = SELECT * FROM vbak WHERE erdat > '20240101';
ENDMETHOD.

" Follow-up: How to handle NULL in SQLScript
METHOD null_safe_amdp
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING lfa1 bsik.
  et_result = SELECT i.lifnr,
                     COALESCE(v.name1, 'Unknown') AS vendor_name,
                     COALESCE(i.dmbtr, 0.00)      AS amount
              FROM bsik AS i
              LEFT JOIN lfa1 AS v ON i.lifnr = v.lifnr
              WHERE i.bukrs = :iv_bukrs;
ENDMETHOD.

" Follow-up: AMDP calling another AMDP
METHOD parent_amdp
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
    USING vbak zcl_helper_class=>helper_method.
  CALL 'ZCL_HELPER_CLASS=>HELPER_METHOD'(
    IN  iv_year  => :iv_year,
    OUT et_rates => lt_rates );
  et_result = SELECT v.vbeln, v.netwr * r.rate AS converted
              FROM vbak AS v
              INNER JOIN :lt_rates AS r ON v.waerk = r.curr;
ENDMETHOD.
amdp-hana-019.interviewScriptHinglish: interviews mein AMDP follow-up questions typically broader ecosystem explore karte hain. BY DATABASE PROCEDURE DML support karta hai jabki BY DATABASE FUNCTION strictly read-only hota hai aur CDS Table Functions implement kar sakta hai. AMDP RAP mein determinations, validations, aur queries ke through fit hota hai. HANA calculation views ko AMDP ke andar USING clause se proper schema qualification ke saath access kiya ja sakta hai. Transport pe, SAP target system mein class activation ke part ke roop mein underlying stored procedure ko automatically regenerate karta hai. Exception handling cx_amdp_error subclasses se hoti hai. Yeh surrounding topics jaanna experience ki breadth signal karta hai aur interviews mein AMDP ko S/4HANA architecture se connect karne mein help karta hai.
amdp-hana-019.interviewScriptEnglish: AMDP follow-up questions in interviews typically explore the broader ecosystem. BY DATABASE PROCEDURE supports DML while BY DATABASE FUNCTION is strictly read-only and can implement CDS Table Functions. AMDP fits into RAP through determinations, validations, and queries. HANA calculation views can be accessed inside AMDP via the USING clause with proper schema qualification. On transport, SAP automatically regenerates the underlying stored procedure in the target system as part of class activation. Exception handling uses cx_amdp_error subclasses. Knowing these surrounding topics signals breadth of experience and helps connect AMDP to S/4HANA architecture in interviews.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between BY DATABASE PROCEDURE and BY DATABASE FUNCTION in AMDP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: BY DATABASE PROCEDURE standard AMDP methods ke liye use hota hai jo directly ABAP code se called hain. Method ki ABAP class signature mein IMPORTING aur EXPORTING parameters defined hain. BY DATABASE FUNCTION specifically tab use hota hai jab AMDP method CDS table function implement karta hai — return structure ABAP method signature ke bajaye CDS DDL ke through define hoti hai. Agar CDS DDL kehti hai 'implemented by method ZCL_X=>MY_METHOD,' us method ko BY DATABASE FUNCTION use karna chahiye, BY DATABASE PROCEDURE nahi.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: BY DATABASE PROCEDURE is used for standard AMDP methods called directly from ABAP code. The method has IMPORTING and EXPORTING parameters defined in the ABAP class signature. BY DATABASE FUNCTION is used specifically when the AMDP method implements a CDS table function — the return structure is defined by the CDS DDL rather than the ABAP method signature. If a CDS DDL says 'implemented by method ZCL_X=>MY_METHOD,' that method must use BY DATABASE FUNCTION, not BY DATABASE PROCEDURE.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Pehli baar CDS table function banate waqt PROCEDURE use kiya tha — activation error aaya: 'Method must be defined as database function.' Change kiya BY DATABASE PROCEDURE ko BY DATABASE FUNCTION mein — activate hua. Yeh ek specific error hai jo is difference ko clearly indicate karta hai. Ab hamesha first check karta hoon: CDS table function? -> FUNCTION. Direct ABAP call? -> PROCEDURE.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: CDS table function implement karne wali method BY DATABASE PROCEDURE ke saath define karna — activation failure. Error message zyada clear nahi hota — isliye yeh distinction yaad rakhna important hai before activation attempt.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BY DATABASE PROCEDURE - standard AMDP (ABAP call)
CLASS zcl_standard_amdp IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_sales
      BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB  " <- PROCEDURE
      LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
      USING vbak.
    et_result = SELECT * FROM vbak
                WHERE erdat > '20240101';
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

" BY DATABASE FUNCTION - for CDS Table Function impl
CLASS zcl_cds_backed_amdp IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_periods
      BY DATABASE FUNCTION FOR HDB  " <- FUNCTION
      LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
      USING t009b.
    et_result = SELECT monat, budat
                FROM t009b
                WHERE gjahr = :p_year;
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
" CDS DDL: implemented by method ZCL_CDS_BACKED_AMDP=>GET_PERIODS

followupAnswerBank.question: How does AMDP fit into the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) model?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: RAP model mein, AMDP primarily read operations ke liye use hota hai — complex database-side calculations jo RAP business object ka data retrieval power karte hain. AMDP methods RAP behavior implementation classes ke andar actions, validations, ya determinations mein computationally intensive logic ke liye call ho sakti hain. AMDP se backed CDS table functions RAP service definition mein underlying projection ki tarah serve kar sakti hain. Lekin RAP ka managed scenario apne own framework se CRUD handle karta hai — RAP mein AMDP write operations ke liye use nahi hota, sirf complex analytical ya calculation logic ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In the RAP model, AMDP is primarily used for read operations — complex database-side calculations that power the RAP business object's data retrieval. AMDP methods can be called inside RAP behavior implementation classes within actions, validations, or determinations for computationally intensive logic. CDS table functions backed by AMDP can serve as the underlying projection in a RAP service definition. However, RAP's managed scenario handles CRUD with its own framework — AMDP is not used for write operations in RAP, only for complex analytical or calculation logic.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: RAP-based app mein inventory valuation complex calculation thi. RAP entity ke read side — AMDP method called kiya jo multi-step SQLScript se valuation compute karta tha. RAP framework returned result expose karta tha OData V4 se. AMDP ka calculation engine tha, RAP framework ka service layer tha — clean separation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: RAP mein AMDP ke saath DML karne ki koshish karna — RAP managed scenario mein framework kI state management bypass hoti hai. AMDP RAP mein primarily read/calculate ke liye use karo, write operations RAP behavior implementations mein karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" RAP Behavior Implementation using AMDP
CLASS lhc_inventory IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_valuation.  " RAP action/read handler
    " Call AMDP for complex calculation
    DATA lo_amdp TYPE REF TO zcl_inventory_amdp.
    DATA lt_valuation TYPE zcl_inventory_amdp=>tt_valuation.
    lo_amdp = NEW zcl_inventory_amdp( ).
    lo_amdp->calculate_valuation(
      IMPORTING iv_plant  = ls_key-plant
      EXPORTING et_result = lt_valuation ).
    " Map AMDP result to RAP result structure
    LOOP AT lt_valuation INTO DATA(ls_val).
      result = VALUE #( BASE result
        ( %key-plant    = ls_val-werks
          valuation_amt = ls_val-total ) ).
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
" AMDP handles: complex DB calculation (pushdown)
" RAP handles: OData exposure, CRUD framework, auth

followupAnswerBank.question: Can AMDP access HANA calculation views?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, AMDP SAP HANA calculation views ko access kar sakta hai unhe USING clause mein full path format ke saath reference karke: '_SYS_BIC'.'package_name/view_name'. SQLScript body ke andar, calculation view phir data source ke roop mein accessible hota hai. Yeh combination powerful hai — calculation views complex analytical models with hierarchies aur star schemas provide karte hain, jabki AMDP upar additional programmatic processing add karta hai. Key dependency consideration yeh hai ki HANA calculation views ABAP transports ke through transport nahi hote aur har system pe separately deploy karne padte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, AMDP can access SAP HANA calculation views by referencing them in the USING clause with the full path format: '_SYS_BIC'.'package_name/view_name'. Inside the SQLScript body, the calculation view is then accessible as a data source. This combination is powerful — calculation views provide complex analytical models with hierarchies and star schemas, while AMDP adds additional programmatic processing on top. The key dependency consideration is that HANA calculation views are not transported via ABAP transports and must be separately deployed to each system.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: BW-based reporting project mein HANA calculation view tha for cost center hierarchy. AMDP mein yeh view access kiya — USING '_SYS_BIC'.'ZFINANCE/ZCOSTCENTER_HIER'. SQLScript mein JOIN kiya with FI line items. Transport mein ABAP class transport + calculation view separate BW transport — coordination zaruri thi. Design doc mein yeh dependency explicitly mention kiya tha.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Calculation view ko ABAP transport mein include karne ki koshish karna — possible nahi. Calculation view BW/HANA workbench se deploy hoti hai. AMDP transport + calculation view deployment separate steps hain — project plan mein dono coordinate karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" AMDP accessing HANA Calculation View
CLASS zcl_fin_reporting_amdp IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_cost_center_data
      BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
      LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
      USING bseg
             "_SYS_BIC"."ZFINANCE/ZCOSTCENTER_HIER".
    " Join FI line items with HANA calculation view
    lt_base = SELECT b.kostl, b.dmbtr, b.bukrs
              FROM bseg AS b
              WHERE b.gjahr = :iv_gjahr
                AND b.bukrs = :iv_bukrs;
    et_result = SELECT l.kostl, h.kostl_text,
                       h.parent_kostl, SUM(l.dmbtr) AS amount
              FROM :lt_base AS l
              INNER JOIN "_SYS_BIC"."ZFINANCE/ZCOSTCENTER_HIER"
                AS h ON l.kostl = h.kostl
              GROUP BY l.kostl, h.kostl_text, h.parent_kostl;
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
" Note: '_SYS_BIC' schema = where HANA calc views live
" Deployment: ABAP class via SE09/SE10
"             Calc view via BW transport or HANA Studio

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens to the AMDP stored procedure when the class is transported to another system?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab AMDP class target system mein import aur activate hoti hai, SAP automatically activation ke dauran corresponding HANA stored procedure create karta hai. Procedure ko separately transport karne ki zarurat nahi — yeh ABAP class source se regenerated hoti hai. Lekin USING clause mein listed sab objects class activate hone se pehle target system mein exist karne chahiye. Agar koi USING-clause dependency — DDIC type, CDS view, ya HANA calculation view — missing hai, activation fail hota hai aur procedure create nahi hoti.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When an AMDP class is imported and activated in a target system, SAP automatically creates the corresponding HANA stored procedure during activation. The procedure does not need to be transported separately — it is regenerated from the ABAP class source. However, all objects listed in the USING clause must exist in the target system before the class can be activated. If any USING-clause dependency — DDIC type, CDS view, or HANA calculation view — is missing, activation fails and the procedure is not created.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: QA system mein AMDP class import hua but activate nahi hua — USING clause mein ek CDS view missing thi. HANA mein koi procedure nahi bani. CDS view import kiya — phir class manually activate kiya SE24 mein — procedure HANA mein ban gayi. Is incident ke baad humne transport sequence documentation mandatory kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Yeh expect karna ki class import hone se automatically activate bhi ho jaayegi — sometimes manual activation zaruri hoti hai agar prerequisites pehle se activate nahi hain. SE24 mein class open karo aur activate karo manually agar automatic nahi hua.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" AMDP Transport Lifecycle:

" Source System:
" 1. Developer creates ZCL_VENDOR_AGING_AMDP in SE24
" 2. Class activated -> HANA procedure created automatically
" 3. Class added to transport request DEVK900123

" Transport Import in Target System:
" Import sequence matters:
" Step 1: Import DEVK900120 (DDIC types) -> activate
" Step 2: Import DEVK900121 (CDS views) -> activate
" Step 3: Import DEVK900123 (AMDP class) -> activate
" On activation: SAP creates stored procedure in HANA

" Verify procedure exists after import:
" HANA Database Explorer:
SELECT procedure_name FROM procedures
WHERE procedure_name LIKE
  '%ZCL_VENDOR_AGING_AMDP%';
" Expected: 1 row found = transport + activation successful
" 0 rows = activation failed, check transport log

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle exceptions in AMDP methods?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP SQLScript ke andar, exceptions SIGNAL statement ke through raise hoti hain — SIGNAL SQLSTATE '75000' SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'custom message'. Yeh HANA-level exception ABAP layer ko cx_amdp_error exception ke roop mein propagate hoti hai. Calling ABAP code isko standard TRY-CATCH block ke through catch karta hai. AMDP method signature mein RAISING cx_amdp_error include karna chahiye is possibility ko declare karne ke liye. Yeh proper exception handling pattern hai — ABAP-style RAISE EXCEPTION SQLScript ke andar use nahi ho sakta.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Inside AMDP SQLScript, exceptions are raised using the SIGNAL statement — SIGNAL SQLSTATE '75000' SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'custom message'. This HANA-level exception is propagated to the ABAP layer as a cx_amdp_error exception. The calling ABAP code catches it using a standard TRY-CATCH block. The AMDP method signature should include RAISING cx_amdp_error to declare this possibility. This is the proper exception handling pattern — ABAP-style RAISE EXCEPTION cannot be used inside SQLScript.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP mein ek validation thi — agar iv_bukrs empty pass kiya toh meaningful error dena. SQLScript mein: IF :iv_bukrs = '' THEN SIGNAL SQLSTATE '75001' SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Company code is required'; END IF; ABAP mein cx_amdp_error catch karke user-friendly message show kiya. Clean error handling.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AMDP method mein RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_custom... likhne ki koshish karna — ABAP statement hai, SQLScript allowed nahi. SIGNAL statement SQLScript ka error mechanism hai. Aur RAISING clause method signature mein add karna bhoolna — caller mein unhandled exception warning aata hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Exception handling in AMDP:

CLASS zcl_amdp_with_exception DEFINITION PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES: if_amdp_marker_hdb.
    METHODS: get_data
      IMPORTING VALUE(iv_bukrs) TYPE bukrs
      EXPORTING VALUE(et_result) TYPE ztt_result
      RAISING   cx_amdp_error.  " <- declare exception
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_amdp_with_exception IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_data
      BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
      LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
      USING bseg.
    " Validate input in SQLScript
    IF :iv_bukrs = '' THEN
      SIGNAL SQLSTATE '75001'  " HANA-level error
        SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Company code required';
    END IF;
    et_result = SELECT * FROM bseg
                WHERE bukrs = :iv_bukrs;
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

" ABAP calling code:
TRY.
  lo_amdp->get_data(
    IMPORTING iv_bukrs  = lv_bukrs
    EXPORTING et_result = lt_result ).
CATCH cx_amdp_error INTO DATA(lx_err).
  MESSAGE lx_err->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
ENDTRY.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the impact of AMDP on unit testing strategy?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP unit testing ko connected HANA database chahiye kyunki SQLScript database layer pe execute hota hai — pure ABAP mocks AMDP execution ko substitute nahi kar sakte. ABAP Unit test classes AMDP methods call kar sakti hain aur results assert kar sakti hain, lekin tests integration-style tests ki tarah run hote hain test system database require karte hue. Approach hai: test system mein controlled test data create karo, test class mein AMDP method call karo, result validation ke liye CL_ABAP_UNIT_ASSERT use karo. ABAP frameworks ke through AMDP test doubles complex hain — most projects controlled datasets ke saath real database tests use karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: AMDP unit testing requires a connected HANA database because the SQLScript executes at the database layer — pure ABAP mocks cannot substitute AMDP execution. ABAP Unit test classes can call AMDP methods and assert the results, but the tests run as integration-style tests requiring the test system database. The approach is: create controlled test data in the test system, call the AMDP method in the test class, use CL_ABAP_UNIT_ASSERT for result validation. AMDP test doubles via ABAP frameworks are complex — most projects use real database tests with controlled datasets.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP ke liye test class banaya — SETUP mein test data insert kiya relevant tables mein, test method mein AMDP call kiya, result assert kiya, TEARDOWN mein data delete kiya. Yeh integration test tha but deterministic — known data in, known result expected. Sab 30 test cases pass hone ka confidence tha before transport.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AMDP ko completely skip karna unit test coverage se — 'test karna mushkil hai toh test nahi karte' approach wrong hai. Integration-style tests with controlled data feasible hain aur necessary hain for quality.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ABAP Unit Test for AMDP Method
CLASS ztcl_vendor_aging_amdp DEFINITION
  FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA mo_amdp TYPE REF TO zcl_vendor_aging_amdp.
    METHODS:
      setup,
      teardown,
      test_basic_aging FOR TESTING,
      test_bucket_30_days FOR TESTING.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ztcl_vendor_aging_amdp IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD setup.
    mo_amdp = NEW zcl_vendor_aging_amdp( ).
    " Insert controlled test data into BSIK
    INSERT bsik FROM TABLE @(
      VALUE #( ( lifnr = 'TESTV001'
                 bukrs = '9999'  " test company code
                 dmbtr = 1000
                 bldat = cl_abap_context_info=>
                         get_system_date( ) - 25 ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD test_basic_aging.
    DATA lt_result TYPE zcl_vendor_aging_amdp=>tt_result.
    mo_amdp->get_vendor_aging(
      IMPORTING iv_bukrs = '9999'
      EXPORTING et_result = lt_result ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial(
      act = lt_result msg = 'Expected results' ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD teardown.
    DELETE FROM bsik WHERE bukrs = '9999'.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does AMDP performance compare to Open SQL with FOR ALL ENTRIES?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FOR ALL ENTRIES driving internal table ke basis pe data ko application server pe multiple batches mein fetch karta hai — LOOP ke andar SELECT ke compared mein DB calls reduce karta hai lekin abhi bhi sab matching rows application server pe transfer karta hai. AMDP sab processing HANA mein rakhta hai — sirf final aggregated result application server pe travel karta hai. Complex processing wale bade data volumes ke liye, AMDP FOR ALL ENTRIES ko dramatically outperform karta hai. Small datasets ya simple lookups ke liye, FOR ALL ENTRIES implement karna simpler hai aur isme koi AMDP instantiation overhead nahi hai. Decision point data volume aur processing complexity hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FOR ALL ENTRIES fetches data to the application server in multiple batches based on a driving internal table — it reduces DB calls compared to SELECT inside a LOOP but still transfers all matching rows to the application server. AMDP keeps all processing in HANA — only the final aggregated result travels to the application server. For large data volumes with complex processing, AMDP dramatically outperforms FOR ALL ENTRIES. For small datasets or simple lookups, FOR ALL ENTRIES is simpler to implement and has no AMDP instantiation overhead. The decision point is data volume and processing complexity.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Report tha — 200,000 open items, joining with vendor master, aggregating by vendor. FOR ALL ENTRIES approach: 200,000 rows app server pe, LOOP mein aggregate — 3 minutes. AMDP: same JOIN + GROUP BY in HANA — 200,000 rows processed at HANA, 150 aggregated rows returned — 6 seconds. Clear winner. But a report fetching 500 items — FOR ALL ENTRIES was simpler and perfectly adequate.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AMDP ko har jagah use karna sirf kyunki yeh 'modern' hai — FOR ALL ENTRIES aur simple SELECTs pragmatic solutions hain chhote data ke liye. Over-engineering avoidance karo. AMDP ka overhead — class instantiation, additional complexity — small data ke liye net negative ho sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FOR ALL ENTRIES — suitable for small-medium data
SELECT vbeln, kunnr FROM vbak
  INTO TABLE @lt_orders
  WHERE vkorg = '1000'.
" Then fetch related:
IF lt_orders IS NOT INITIAL.
  SELECT vbeln, matnr, netwr FROM vbap
    INTO TABLE @lt_items
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_orders
    WHERE vbeln = @lt_orders-vbeln.
ENDIF.
" Result: rows come to app server, process in ABAP
" OK for < 10,000 rows, simple logic

" AMDP — optimal for large data, complex processing
METHOD large_volume_processing
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING vbak vbap.
  lt_orders = SELECT a.vbeln, a.kunnr
              FROM vbak AS a WHERE a.vkorg = '1000';
  et_result = SELECT o.kunnr, SUM(p.netwr) AS total
              FROM :lt_orders AS o
              INNER JOIN vbap AS p ON o.vbeln = p.vbeln
              GROUP BY o.kunnr;
  " Result: only aggregated rows return to app server
  " Optimal for > 50,000 rows, complex aggregation
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the SERIES_GENERATE_DATE function and when is it used in AMDP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SERIES_GENERATE_DATE ek HANA built-in table function hai jo start aur end date ke beech specified interval — day, month, quarter — pe continuous date series generate karta hai. AMDP mein yeh time-series reporting ke liye use hota hai jahan tumhe sab periods dikhane hote hain chahe certain periods ke liye data exist na kare. Iske bina, actual data pe GROUP BY zero transactions wale months skip kar deta hai. Generated date series ke against actual data ko LEFT JOIN karke, every period result mein appear karta hai — missing periods ke liye zero dikhane ke liye COALESCE ke saath.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SERIES_GENERATE_DATE is a HANA built-in table function that generates a continuous series of dates between a start and end date at a specified interval — day, month, quarter. It is used in AMDP for time-series reporting where you need to show all periods even when no data exists for certain periods. Without it, a GROUP BY on actual data skips months with zero transactions. By LEFT JOINing actual data against the generated date series, every period appears in the result — with COALESCE to show zero for missing periods.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Monthly sales report tha — client ne dekha ki March mein sales zero thi toh row hi nahi aayi report mein. Fix: SERIES_GENERATE_DATE se 12 months generate kiye, LEFT JOIN sales data se — March zero ke saath aaya. Client happy. Yeh pattern time-series completeness ke liye standard approach hai HANA mein.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SERIES_GENERATE_DATE output directly EXPORTING karne ki koshish karna without JOIN — function result directly table nahi hoti AMDP EXPORTING ke liye, tabular variable mein capture karo first. Syntax: lt_dates = SELECT generated_period_start FROM SERIES_GENERATE_DATE(...);
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SERIES_GENERATE_DATE for complete time-series reporting
METHOD get_monthly_sales
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING vbak.
  DECLARE lv_start DATE := ADD_YEARS(CURRENT_DATE, -1);
  DECLARE lv_end   DATE := CURRENT_DATE;
  " Generate all months in the last year
  lt_months = SELECT SERIES_ELEMENT AS month_start
              FROM SERIES_GENERATE_DATE(
                'INTERVAL 1 MONTH',
                :lv_start,
                :lv_end );
  " Aggregate actual sales by month
  " Stable bucket: first day of erdat's month
  lt_actual = SELECT ADD_DAYS(erdat,
                       1 - DAYOFMONTH(erdat)) AS mth,
                     SUM(netwr) AS revenue
              FROM vbak WHERE erdat >= :lv_start
              GROUP BY
                ADD_DAYS(erdat, 1 - DAYOFMONTH(erdat));
  " LEFT JOIN: every month shown, zero where no sales
  et_result = SELECT m.month_start,
                     COALESCE(a.revenue, 0.00) AS revenue
              FROM :lt_months AS m
              LEFT JOIN :lt_actual AS a
                ON m.month_start = a.mth
              ORDER BY m.month_start;
ENDMETHOD.
